Objective
Under New York’s FAST NY Shovel-Ready Grant Program, Empire State Development will provide up to $400 million in grants to prepare and develop sites statewide to jumpstart New York’s shovel-readiness and increase its attractiveness to large employers, including high-tech manufacturing, particularly semiconductor manufacturing, interstate distribution and logistics businesses. The program will help diversify New York State’s economy while propelling new investments for businesses, communities and job creation.
Next Section
Continue
Overview
Under the Program, ESD provides grants for pre-development activities and infrastructure investments to develop sites that will attract many eligible industries —including, but need not be limited to, high-tech manufacturing, clean-tech renewable energy, life sciences, agribusiness, optics, transportation equipment, materials processing, industrial machinery manufacturing and other advanced manufacturing. These sites can also be used for interstate distribution and logistics. However, ESD may give priority to semiconductor manufacturing projects and related industry and supply chain projects. The Program further provides ESD the ability to award a “FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification,” for sites that meet high standards of shovel readiness, to support and enhance their marketing efforts to attract many more industries.
Track A - FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification
This no-cost designation by ESD for site-marketing purposes establishes that the applicant has worked proactively with New York State (the “State”) to address all major permitting issues prior to a business expressing interest in the location.
Track B - FAST NY Pre-Development Grants
Working capital grants of up to $500,000 per site will be awarded to fund the cost of necessary pre-development site planning and reviews for future shovel-ready sites.
Grants will fund necessary costs to assist grantees in completing pre-development activities, such as those required for FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification.
Track C - FAST NY Infrastructure Improvements
Capital grants will be awarded to improve the shovel-readiness of existing, mature sites by funding infrastructure improvements such as water, sewer, transportation, electricity, gas and other capital-eligible site improvement costs.
Competitive grant awards will be available to sites that have substantially met the requirements of FAST NY Shovel-Ready certification.
Next Section
Continue
Program Status
*As of December 2025
Since formally launching in December 2022, the FAST NY program has been working to create Shovel Ready Sites throughout New York State. The program has made the following announcements:
1st round of grant awards in
August 2023
2nd round in
November 2023
3rd round in
March 2024
4th round in
October 2024
5th round in
February 2025
6th round in
August 2025
7th round in
December 2025
Combined awards total over $326 million for 42 projects across the state and improving nearly 8,800 acres of industrial property. Awarded projects include both pre-development (Track B) projects totaling over $5 million and infrastructure improvement (Track C) projects totaling over $321 million.
Actively accepting Letters of Intent and Applications for Track B and Track C grants.

Eligibility
Click here to view FAST NY Guidelines
Eligible Applicants:
Municipalities
- a municipal subdivision that is a county, city, town, or village in New York State
Municipally designated non-profit economic development organizations
- New York State-based nonprofit economic development corporations designated to act on the behalf of the municipality in which the site is located.
Municipally designated Local Development Corporation/Industrial Development Agency
– New York State-based nonprofit industrial development agencies and authorities designated to act on the behalf of the municipality in which the site is located
Site Condition Eligibility:
Sites must be at least 40 acres in size to be eligible for grant consideration. Exceptions may be made for smaller sites demonstrating compelling site assets or public benefits, including, but need not be limited to: proximity to intermodal transportation assets, such as transportation terminals; population centers; the possibility for brownfield redevelopment.
Applicant must either
have ownership or control of the site, or
provide written consent from the ownership entity and, if different, the entity that has legal control of the site, to the application for FAST NY funding and, if awarded, agreeing to use FAST NY funds as outlined in the application.

How to Apply
FAST NY Program Guidelines
Intent to Apply Form

CFA Application
Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis through the CFA portal.
After ESD receives an Intent to Apply form, applicants will receive an email confirming receipt within 7 calendar days. A determination regarding eligibility to submit the full application will be made within 30 calendar days. Full applications will not be accepted until applicants have received an approved Letter of Intent to Apply from ESD.
For additional information on the application process, please see detailed program information using the instructions above and/or contact your regional office.
Next Section
Continue
Documentation Requirements
This section outlines the documentation necessary to be considered for an award, broken down by Award Track. Please note that additional documents may be requested in order to complete ESD’s evaluation of applications.
Track A – FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification
ESD will award FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification to those applicants that satisfy the production requirement of the following documents, as applicable:
State Historic Preservation Office (SHPO) consultation on plan
Site plan and subdivision approval by local municipality
Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA) and/or Phase II ESA report
Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) mapping
Park rules, regulations and covenants document (created by developer)
NYS Dept of Environmental Conservation (DEC) Natural Heritage Program correspondence and U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS) database search findings
Environmental Assessment Form (EAF) and Negative Declaration or Draft & Final Environmental Impact Statement (FEIS)
Site plan and boundary survey map
Soil survey map
Signoff by oversight body if needed for special district (e.g., Adirondack Park Agency, agricultural district, etc.)
Preliminary storm water management plan
NYS Department of Transportation (DOT) review of traffic impact study
Wetland avoidance and minimization plan - pre-application meeting with US Army Corps of Engineers and DEC
Smart Growth Impact Statement
Other necessary project approvals specific to site
Track B – FAST NY Pre-Development Grants
The following documents are required to be considered for a FAST NY Pre-Development grant:
Letter of Intent with an executive summary of the applicant, proposed property, proposed outcome, proposed funding request, and how the funding will be used to develop a FAST NY-certified Shovel-Ready Site.
Letter of support from the chief executive and/or legislative resolution of the local municipality that maintains land use control over the proposed project site.
Detailed plan to market the FAST NY site for the intended industries.
Documentation of site ownership or control or written consent from the ownership entity consenting to the application for FAST NY funding.
Other materials as may be required under a FAST NY program application.
Track C – FAST NY Capital Infrastructure Improvements
The following documents are required to be considered for a FAST NY Capital Infrastructure Improvements grant:
Letter of support from the chief executive and/or legislative resolution of the local municipality that maintains land use control over the proposed project site.
Documentation of site ownership or control or written consent from the ownership entity consenting to the application for FAST NY funding.
Documented research and/or market study showing the specific industry that will be targeted and the specific infrastructure needs. Applicant must demonstrate that the requested infrastructure will serve to help attract the specified economic development.
Detailed cost estimates for capital upgrades.
Documentation of engagement with water and sewer districts, transportation departments, and local utility providers for needed infrastructure. A detailed and documented plan for how infrastructure upgrade(s) will be accomplished is required, including a willingness of the necessary parties.
Other materials as may be required under a FAST NY program application.
Next Section
Continue
Additional Information
Additional Award Criteria to be Considered by ESD
In addition to meeting documentation requirements outlined in Section VI of the Program Guidelines, ESD will consider the following criteria when evaluating applications.
Track B (FAST NY Pre-Development Grants) & Track C (FAST NY Capital Infrastructure Improvements)
Demonstration of industry interest.
Demonstration of financial viability.
Alignment with Regional Economic Development Council’s regional economic development strategy.
Leveraged investment, such as private and local investment.
For Track C only, tenant commitments or strong indications of interest, including but not limited to signed letters of interest and/or contributions.
For Track B only, commitment to substantially complete all requirements of FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification by the completion of the Pre-Development Grant shall be required.
For Track C only, substantial completion of all requirements of FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification prior to being awarded a Capital Infrastructure Improvement grant shall be required.
Additional Information
Up to $200 million will be made available for capital grant funding, of which up to 10% may be allocated for working capital grants.
Awards will be allocated on a rolling basis until funding is exhausted.
Projects will initially be reviewed for eligibility, may be subject to site visit, and will ultimately be selected for advancement or denial based on an internal project review committee.
In the case of Capital Infrastructure Improvement grants, as part of its grantmaking, ESD may require restrictions on the future use of awarded sites consistent with the purposes of their FAST NY grant terms.
A one percent (1%) non-reimbursable commitment fee based on the grant awarded, will be assessed to all awardees.

 I. Overview  
 
On February 28, 2022, Governor Kathy Hochul announced that the Fiscal Year 2023 New 
York State Budget would make available up to $200 million for Empire State 
Development (“ESD”) to fund the new Focused Attraction of Shovel -Ready Tracts New 
York (FAST NY) Grant Program (the “Program”) . The Program received an additional $100 
million through the Fiscal Year 2024 New York State Budget, for a total of $300 million in 
FAST NY funding. Under the Program, ESD will provide grants for pre -development 
activities and  infrastructure investments to develop sites that will attract high -tech 
manufacturing, particularly semiconductor manufacturing, warehousing, distribution 
and logistics businesses to the state to jumpstart New York's shovel -readiness and 
increase the state's attractiveness to large emp loyers. The Program further provides ESD 
the ability to award a “FAST NY Site” certification for sites that meet high standards of 
shovel readiness to support their marketing to industry.  
 
II. Definitions  
 
▪ MUNICIPALITY shall mean a municipal subdivision that is a county, city, town, or village  
in New York State .  
▪ MUNICIPALLY DESIGNATED NON -PROFIT ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT ORGANIZATIONS  
shall mean New York State -based  nonprofit  industrial development agencies and 
authorities  and economic development corporations  designated to act on the behalf of 
the municipality in which the site is located . 
▪ SHOVEL -READY SITE shall mean a land area in New York State that has been prepared 
for future construction.  
 
III. Eligib ility Criteria  
 
Eligible Applicants : 
▪ Municipalities  
▪ Municipally designated non -profit economic development  organizations  
▪ Municipally designated Local Development Corporation/Industrial Development 
Agency  
  
Site Condition  Eligibility : 
▪ Sites must be at least 40 acres in size to be eligible for grant consideration. Exceptions 
may be made for smaller sites demonstrating compelling site assets or public 
benefits, including but not limited to : proximity to intermodal transportation assets, 
such as transportation terminals ; population centers ; the possibility for brownfield 
redevelopment . 
▪ Applicant  must either  
o have  ownership or control of the site, or  
o provide written consent from the ownership entity and, if different, the 
entity that has legal control of the site , to the application for FAST NY 
funding and, if awarded, agreeing to use FAST NY funds as outlined in the 
application.  

4  
 
 IV. Awards  
 
Track A - FAST NY Shovel -Ready Certification  
▪ This no -cost designation by ESD for site marketing purposes establishes that the 
applicant has worked proactively with New York State (the “State”) to address all 
major permitting issues prior to a business expressing interest in the location.  
 
Track B - FAST NY Pre -Development Grants  
▪ Working capital grants of up to $500,000 per site will be awarded to fund the cost of 
necessary pre -development site planning and reviews for future shovel ready sites. 
(Mega -sites over a certain acreage and/or large power users may qualify for funding 
up to $1,000,000 per site , based on compelling site assets or public benefits.)  
▪ Grants will fund necessary costs to assist grantees in completing pre -development 
activities, such as those required for FAST NY Shovel -Ready Certification.  
 
Track C - FAST NY Infrastructure Improvements  
▪ Capital grants will be awarded to improve the shovel -readiness of existing, mature 
sites by funding infrastructure improvements such as water, sewer, transportation, 
electricity, gas  and other capital -eligible site improvement costs .  
▪ Competitive grant awards will be available to  sites that have substantially met the 
requirements of  FAST NY  Shovel -Ready certifi cation . 
 
V. Eligible Use of Grant Funds  
 
▪ Eligible industries include : 
o high-tech manufacturing, including but not limited to  clean -tech  renewable 
energy, life sciences , agribusiness, optics,  transportation equipment, 
materials processing, industrial machinery manufacturing  and other 
advanced manufacturing . 
o sites for warehousing, distribution, and logistics . 
▪ Priority may be given to semiconductor manufacturing projects  and related industry 
and supply -chain projects .  
 
Track B – FAST NY Pre -Development Grants : Grantees will be permitted to use funds for the 
following:  
▪ professional services or consulting fees for engineering, environmental assessment, 
surveying, and planning directly related to development of the site.  
▪ professional services incurred for site layout, plotting, and design work.  
▪ conducting archeological and historic surveys, assessments, and studies.  
▪ performing soil sampling; test borings, compaction tests, etc.  
▪ professional services for the cost of engineering and design related to installation or 
upgrades to public infrastructure including water, sewer, electric, and gas service, 
highway access, interior roads, and other improvements.  
▪ other activities approved by ESD as necessary to achieve FAST NY Shovel -Ready 
Certification.  
 

5  
 
 Track C – FAST NY Capital Infrastructure Improvements : Grantees will be permitted to use 
funds for the following:  
▪ investments in water  infrastructure , sewer  infrastructure , roads, electricity  
(including power lines and electrical substations),  natural gas  lines, specialized 
telecommunications infrastructure, and other site infrastructure.  
▪ acquisition of real property  and capital -eligible site preparation and development 
expenses . 
 
VI. Application Instructions  
 
Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis.  
 
Letter of Intent  
• A “Letter of Intent” demonstrating an intent to apply is required. Information 
regarding the Letter of Intent is available online at: https://esd.ny.gov/fast -ny. 
• ESD will confirm receipt by email within 7 calendar days.  
• Within 30 calendar days, ESD will advise applicant by email whether they may proceed 
with the full application.  
 Application  
• Application documentation requirements are broken down by track in Section VII 
below.  
• For Tracks B and C, a $250 non -refundable application fee will be required prior to 
application submission. Documentation of this payment is required as a part of the 
application.  
• Eligible applicants must complete the application through the Consolidated Funding 
Application (CFA) online portal: https://apps.cio.ny.gov/apps/cfa/ . 
• Once submitted, ESD will review applications for completeness and will thereafter 
score applications.  
• Site visits will be completed prior to award selection.  
• Projects will ultimately be selected for advancement or denial by an internal project 
review committee.  
• Awardees will be notified by email. Thereafter, a loan and grants project manager 
will send a grant disbursement agreement to the applicant.  
Commitment Fee  
• A one percent (1%), non -reimbursable  commitment fee based on the grant 
amount awarded will be assessed to all awardees.  
• The commitment fee will be due when the applicant executes documents 
required for processing the award following approval by ESD Directors.  
• Applicant will be obligated to pay for out -of-pocket expenses incurred by ESD in 
connection with the project, including, but not limited to attorney fees, 
appraisals, surveys, title insurance, credit searches, filing fees, public hearing 
expenses, and othe r requirements deemed appropriate by ESD.  

6  
 
  
VII. Documentation Requirements  for Applicants  
 
This section outlines the documentation necessary to be considered for an award, broken 
down by Award Track. Please note that additional documents may be requested  in order to 
complete ESD’s evaluation of applications.   
 
Track A – FAST NY Certification  
ESD will award FAST NY Certification to those applicants that satisfy the production 
requirement of the following documents , as applicable : 
▪ State Historic Preservation Office ( SHPO ) consultation on plan  
▪ Site plan and subdivision approval  by local municipality  
▪ Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA) and/or Phase II ESA report  
▪ Federal Emergency Management Agency ( FEMA ) mapping  
▪ Park rules, regulations and covenants doc ument (created by developer)  
▪ NYS Dept. of Environmental Conservation (DEC) Natural Heritage program 
correspondence and U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service ( USFWS ) database search 
findings  
▪ Environmental Assessment Form ( EAF) and Negative Declaration  or Draft & Final 
Environmental Impact Statement (FEIS)   
▪ Site plan and boundary survey map   
▪ Soil survey map  
▪ Signoff by oversight body  if needed for special district (e.g., Adirondack Park 
Agency, agricultural district, etc.)  
▪ Preliminary storm water management plan  
▪ NYS Department of Transportation ( DOT ) review of traffic impact  study  
▪ Wetland avoidance  and minimization plan - pre-application meeting with Army 
Corps of Engineers  and DEC  
▪ Other necessary project approvals specific to site  
 
Track B – FAST NY Pre -Development Grants  
The following documents are required to be considered for a FAST NY Pre -Development 
grant:  
▪ Letter of Intent with an executive summary of the applicant, proposed property, 
proposed outcome, proposed funding request, and how the funding will be used to 
develop a FAST NY -certified Shovel -Ready Site.  
▪ Letter of support from the chief executive and/or legislative resolution  of the local 
municipality that maintains land use  control over the proposed project site.  
▪ Detailed plan to market the FAST NY site for the intended industries.  
▪ Documentation of site ownership or control or written consent from the ownership 
entity consenting to the application for FAST NY funding.  
▪ Other materials as may be required under a FAST NY program application.  
  

7  
 
 Track C – FAST NY Capital Infrastructure Improvements  
The following documents are required to be considered for a FAST NY Capital Infrastructure 
Improvements grant:  
▪ Letter of support from the chief executive and/or legislative resolution  of the local 
municipality that maintains land use  control over the proposed project site.  
▪ Documentation of site ownership or control or written consent from the ownership 
entity consenting to the application for FAST NY funding.   
▪ Documented research  and/or market study showing the specific industry that will be 
targeted and the specific infrastructure needs. Applicant must demonstrate that the 
requested infrastructure will serve to help attract the specified economic 
development.  
▪ Detailed c ost estimates for capital upgrades.  
▪ Documentation of engagement with water and sewer districts, transportation 
departments, and local utility providers  for needed infrastructure.  A detailed and 
documented plan for how infrastructure upgrade (s) will be accomplished  is required , 
including a willingness of the necessary parties.  
▪ Other materials as may be required under a FAST NY program application.  
 
VIII. Additional Award Criteria to be Considered by ESD  
 
In addition to meeting documentation requirements outlined in Section VI I of the Program 
Guidelines, ESD will consider the following criteria when evaluating applications .   
 
Track B  (FAST NY Pre -Development Grants) and Track C (FAST NY Capital Infrastructure 
Improvements)  
▪ Demonstration of industry interest.  
▪ Demonstration of financial viability.  
▪ Alignment with R egional Economic Development Council’s  regional economic 
development strategy.  
▪ Leveraged investment, such as private and local investment.  
▪ For Track C only , tenant commitments, including but not limited to signed letters of 
interest and/or contributions.  
▪ For Track B only , commitment to substantially complete all requirements of FAST NY 
Certification by the completion of the Pre -Development Grant  shall be required.  
▪ For Track C only , substantial completion of all requirements of FAST NY Certification 
prior to being awarded a Capital Infrastructure Improvement grant  shall be required.  
 
IX. Non-Discrimination and Contractor & Supplier Diversity  
 
Pursuant to New York State Executive Law Articles 15 -A and Article 3 of New York State 
Veterans’ Services Law, ESD recognizes its obligation under the law to promote opportunities 
for maximum feasible participation of certified minority and women -owned bus iness 
enterprises (MWBEs) and service -disabled veteran -owned businesses (SDVOBs) in the 
performance of ESD projects.  The Recipient shall be required to use Good Faith Efforts 
(pursuant to 5 NYCRR §142.8 and 9 NYCRR § 252.2(m)) to achieve MWBE and SDVOB 
Participation. In addition, ESD’s MWBE and SDVOB Participation/Equal Employment 

8  
 
 Opportunity Policy will apply to this project. Specific goals related to the total value of ESD’s 
funding will be established on a grant -by-grant basis.  
 
X. Additional Information  
 
▪ Up to $ 300 million will be made available for capital grant funding, of which up to 
10% may be allocated for working capital grants.  
▪ Awards will be allocated on a rolling basis until funding is exhausted . 
▪ Projects will initially be reviewed for eligibility, may be subject to site visit, and will 
ultimately be selected for advancement or denial based on an internal project review 
committee.   
▪ In the case of Capital Infrastructure Improvement grants, as part of its grantmaking, 
ESD may require restrictions on the future use of awarded sites consistent with the 
purposes of their FAST NY grant terms.

New York State Utility Economic Development Programs  Aligned 
w/ FAST NY  
These programs are designed to support the development and expansion of industrial sites, 
enhance energy efficiency, and promote the reuse of underutilized properties through various 
forms of financial and infrastructural support.  https://esd.ny.gov/fast -ny 
 
 
Central Hudson Gas & Electric  
 
Service Territory:  Mid-Hudson River Valley  
 
• Infrastructure for Manufacturers Program:  Offers grants of up to $200,000 for utility 
infrastructure improvements and $100,000 for site development needs at vacant or 
underutilized properties.  
https://www.cenhud.com/en/about -us/community/economic -development/infrastructure -
for-manufacturers -program/  
 
• Expansion and Retention for Manufacturers:  Supports manufacturers with grants for 
new capital investments within the  
 service territory. Incentives are structured based on investment amounts, ranging from 
$100,000 to $200,000.  
https://www. cenhud.com/en/about -us/community/economic -development/expansion -and-
retention -for-manufacturers -program/  
 
 
National Grid (Niagara Mohawk Power Corporation, including 
KEDNY and KEDLI)  
 
Service Territory:  Upstate NY, Brooklyn, Queens, Staten Island, and Long Island  
 
• Electric Capital Investment Incentive Program  (Upstate NY ): Provides funds to 
offset costs for upgrading utility infrastructure for business expansions or new 
construction projects. 
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/ElectricCapitalInvestmentIncentive.pd
f 
 
• Building Ready  Upstate  Program  (Upstate NY ): This program is designed to expedite 
new construction and major renovations by providing streamlined services and 
infrastructu re readiness. It reduces project start -up times, provides expedited service 
connections, and offers dedicated support through a project manager. Developers and 
business owners undertaking new construction or major renovations within National 
Grid's service  areas are eligible.  
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/BuildingReadyUpstateProgram.pdf  
 
 

• Urban Center /Commercial District Revitalization Program  (Upstate NY ): Designed 
to enhance urban centers and commercial districts, directly aligning  with initiatives  
focused on improving site readiness for larger development projects . 
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/UrbanCenter.pdf  
 
• Three -Phase  Power Incentive  (Upstate  NY): Supports site  readiness for large projects, 
especially in areas where the existing electrical infrastructure may not support  high-
power  needs.  
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/3phasePowerIncentive.pdf  
 
• Renewable  Energy and Energy and Economic Development  (Upstate NY ): Aim to 
promote the use of alternative generations technologies that generate  significant 
economic development benefits within National Grid’s Upstate New York service 
territory . This program provides matching grants for collaborative projects  involving 
local research institutions, renewable energy partners, and regional economic 
development sponsors.  
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/RenewableEnerg yandEconomicDevel
opment.pdf  
 
• Power Quality Enhancements  Program  (Upstate NY ): Designed to address issues 
related to power quality that can affect production, operations, and the overall 
performance of business.  
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/PowerQualityEnhancementProgram.p
df 
 
• Shovel Ready Incentive Program  (Upstate NY ): Provides funding to help develop 
Shovel -Ready certified sites, with up to $125,000 for planning and $500,000 for 
infrastructure installation. 
https://www.shovelready.com /ProgramDocuments/ShovelReadyIncentive.pdf  
 
• Energy Efficiency  in Empire  Zones  (Upstate NY ): Designed to assist businesses 
located in New York State -designated Empire Zones to enhance their energy efficiency.  
This program provides incentives  for installing  energy -efficient systems and technologies 
such as lighting, HVAC equipment, and controls, which are crucial for reducing  energy  
consumption and enhancing productivity.  
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/EnergyEfficiencyInEmpireZones.pdf  
 
• Electrical Manufacturing Productivity Program  (Upstate NY ): This program 
supports small and medium -sized manufacturers in upstate New York, enhancing their 
global competitiveness through new product development, market expansion, and 
increased sales ROI. It is a collaboration with NY MEP centers across multiple re gions 
and FuzeHub. Small and medium -sized manufacturers in upstate New York facing high 
operational costs and regulatory pressures are eligible.  
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/ElectricManufacturingProductivityPro
gram.pdf  
 

• Natural Gas Capital Investment Incentive ( Upstate NY, KEDNY, and KEDLI ): 
Assists with the costs related to upgrading natural gas infrastructure for business 
expansion, including potential additional funds for employee training.  
https://www.shovelready.com/DownstateProgramDocuments/NaturalGasCapitalInvestme
ntIncentive.pdf  - (KEDNY & KEDLI)  
https://www.shovelready.com/P rogramDocuments/NaturalGasCapitalInvestmentIncentiv
e.pdf  - (Upstate NY)  
 
• Industrial Building Redevelopment ( Upstate NY, KEDNY , and KEDLI ): Provides up 
to $250,000 to property owners who are upgrading infrastructure to reactivate 
underutilized buildings as multi -tenant industrial properties.  
https://www.shovelready.com/DownstateProgramDocuments/IndustrialBuildingRedevelo
pmentProgram.pdf  -  (KEDNY & KEDLI ) 
https://www.shovelready.com/ProgramDocuments/IndustrialBuildingRedevelopmentPro
gram.pdf  - (Upstate NY ) 
 
• Natural Gas Manufacturing Productivity  Program  (Upstate NY,  KEDNY , and 
KEDLI ): Supports productivity improvements in manufacturing through grants  aiding in 
lean manufacturing projects or growth -targeted activities.

and meet at least one of the foll owing requirements: New facility (minimal annual usage  
of 12,000 Ccf), existing facility purchased/leased and vacant 6 months or more (minimal 
annual usage of 12,000 Ccf), expansion of existing facility (minimal annual incremental

programs/

incremental gas -fired equipment at industrial or commercial properties in Western New 
York, enhancing natural gas consumption. Provides funding to reduce the capital cost of 
system improvements, piping, and customer -specific gas -fired equipment. Large 
commercial and industrial customers in Western New York under a required contract  are 
eligible .

programs/

companies in converting to Natural Gas Vehicles (NGVs) by offering financial support 
for the installation of NGV refueling stations and the purchase of new vehicles. 
Companies within National Fuel's service territory planning to adop t NGV technology 
are eligible. Funding  helps reduce the cost of installing NGV refueling stations and 
purchasing NGVs, thus lowering total project payback.  
https://www.nationalfuel.com/utility/benefits -of-natural -gas/financial -assistance -
programs/

and installation of Combined Heat & Power (CHP) equipment, which produces electricity 
and useful heat simultaneously from natural gas. Commercial and industrial energy users 
within National Fuel's service territory, where energy cost and reliability are a concern , 
are eligible. Benefits include financial  aid to help reduce the installation costs of CHP 
systems, enhancing energy efficiency and reliability.

programs/

FAST NY Grant Application Instructions – Track B 
Track A – Shovel-Ready Certification 
Track B – Pre-Development Grants 
Track C – Infrastructure Improvements
Program Overview 
The Fiscal Year 2023 New York State Budget provided funding of up to $200 million for Empire State 
Development (ESD) to fund the new Focused Attraction of Shovel-Ready Tracts New York (FAST NY) 
Grant Program. Under the Program, ESD will provide grants for pre-development activities and 
infrastructure investments to develop sites that will attract high-tech manufacturing, particularly 
semiconductor manufacturing and related industry and supply chain projects, and interstate distribution 
and logistics businesses to the state to jumpstart New York’s shovel-readiness and increase the state’s 
attractiveness to future employers. The program further provides ESD the ability to award a “FAST NY 
Shovel-Ready Certification” for sites meeting high standards of shovel readiness to support their 
marketing to industry. 
Eligibility Criteria 
Eligible Applicants
oMunicipalities
oMunicipally designated non-profit economic development organizations
oMunicipally designated Local Development Corporation/Industrial Development Agency
Site Condition Eligibility
oSites must be 40 acres in size to be eligible to be considered
Exceptions may be made for smaller sites demonstrating compelling site assets 
or public benefits, including but need not be limited to: proximity to intermodal 
transportation assets, such as transportation terminals; population centers; the 
possibility for brownfield redevelopment
oApplicant must have: 
Ownership or control of the site, or 
Written consent from the ownership entity and, if different, the entity that has 
legal control of the site, to the application for FAST NY funding and, if awarded, 
agreeing to use FAST NY funds as outlined in the application 
Award Eligibility 
oTrack B – Pre-development Grants 
Working capital grants of up to $500,000 per site will be awarded to fund the 
cost of necessary pre-development site planning and reviews for future shovel 
ready sites 
Grants will fund necessary costs to assist grantees in completing pre-
development activities, such as those required for FAST NY Shovel-Ready 
Certification 
50% non-ESD match required 
Grant funds are issued on a reimbursement basis 

Expenditures incurred prior to the date of award letter are not eligible for 
reimbursement 
Application Instructions 
Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis 
Letter of Intent to apply 
oA Letter of Intent to apply demonstrating an intent to apply is required 
This can be found on the website under “How to Apply” here: 
https://esd.ny.gov/fast-ny
oESD will confirm receipt by email within 7 calendar days 
oWithin 30 calendar days, ESD will advise applicant by email whether they may proceed 
with the full application 
Application 
oApplication documentation requirements can be found in the checklist below 
oA $250 non-refundable application fee will be required prior to application submission. 
Documentation of this payment is required as a part of the application. A wire transfer 
should be made to Empire State Development Corporation using the information below: 
Bank Name: JPMorgan Chase Bank 
Account Name: ESD ED Income 
ABA Number: 021 000 021 
Account Number: 007004796 
Reference: “[Applicant Name] – FAST NY Application Fee” 
oEligible applicants must complete the application through the Consolidated Funding 
Application (CFA) online portal: https://apps.cio.ny.gov/apps/cfa/
oOnce submitted, ESD will review applications for completeness and will thereafter score 
applications 
oSite visits will be completed prior to award selection 
oProjects will ultimately be selected for advancement or denial based on an internal 
project review committee 
oAwardees will be notified by email. Thereafter, a loan and grants project manager will 
send a grant disbursement agreement to the applicant 
oA one percent (1%) non-reimbursable commitment fee based on the grant amount 
awarded will be assessed to all awardees. The commitment fee will be due when the 
applicant executes documents required for processing the award, following approval by 
ESD Directors. The applicant will be obligated to pay for out-of-pocket expenses 
incurred by ESD in connection with the project, including, but not limited to, expenses 
related to attorney fees, appraisals, surveys, title insurance, credit searches, filing fees, 
public hearing expenses and other requirements deemed appropriate by ESD. 

Application Attachment Checklist
Track B –Documentation Item s Yes N/A
Letter of Intent 
Documentation of site ownership/control, OR
Written consent from the ownership entity consenting to the application for funding 
Letter of support from the chief executive and/or legislative resolution of the local 
municipality that maintains land use control over the proposed project site 
For EDO applicants, a municipal resolution or letter from a municipal executive 
designating the EDO to apply for FAST NY funds 
(this does not apply to IDA’s or EDO’s where the organization’s board is entirely municipally appointed) 
Detailed plan to market the FAST NY site fortheintended industries
Project PowerPoint presentation (15 slides maximum)
(include information on targeted industry, maps of the site, developer experience, funding request, etc.)
Demonstration of industry interest
(ie, studies, leads) 
Demonstration of financial viability
(when applicant is partnering with a privately held entity, please provide their 3 years’ audited financials) 
Demonstration of alignment with R egional Economic Development Council ’s (REDC) 
regional strategy 
Documentation of leveraged investment, such as private and loc al investment
(including the 50% non-ESD match requirement)
Detailed sources and uses of funds table including documentation of leveraged 
investment, such as private and local investment 
(including the 50% non-ESD match requirement) 
Plan to substantially complete FAST NY Shovel -Ready C ertification items
(as detailed in Track A Addendum, see Application Instructions)
Documentation of any environmental review 
Documentation from local planning/zoning
Application fee proof of payment
Eligible Uses of Funds  
Eligible industries: 
oHigh-tech manufacturing, including but need not be limited to clean-tech renewable 
energy, life sciences, agribusiness, optics, transportation equipment, materials 
processing, industrial machinery manufacturing and other advanced manufacturing 
oSites for interstate distribution and logistics 
oPriority may be given to semiconductor manufacturing and related industry and supply-
chain projects  
Eligible site activities:
oManufacturing
oResearch and development
oOffice park
oInterstate distribution and logistics
All grant funds will be issued on a reimbursement basis 

The Recipient shall be required to use “Good Faith Efforts,” to achieve a Minority and Women-
owned Business Enterprise (“MWBE”) participation goal
Grantees will be permitted to use funds for the following: 
oProfessional services or consulting fees for engineering, environmental assessment, 
surveying, and planning directly related to development of the site 
oProfessional services incurred for site layout, plotting, and design work 
oConducting archeological and historic surveys, assessments, and studies 
oPerforming soil sampling, test borings, compaction tests, etc. 
oProfessional services for the cost of engineering and design related to installation or 
upgrades to public infrastructure including water, sewer electric, and gas services, 
highway access, interior roads, and other improvements 
oOther activities approved by ESD as necessary to achieve FAST NY Shovel-Ready 
Certification 
Scoring Criteria 
The following will be evaluated as a part of the application scoring
oDemonstration of industry interest (ie. studies, leads)
oDemonstration of financial viability (when applicant is partnering with a privately held 
entity, please provide their three years audited financials)
oAlignment with REDC strategy
oLeveraged investment (match)
oCommitment and plan to substantially complete FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification 
items (see Track A addendum)
oFavorable site characteristics (topography, developability, useable acreage, etc)
oHighway and truck access to meet needs of intended use
oApplicant’s development experience
oUtility services to site to meet needs of intended use
oRail service, barge access or airport access to meet needs of intended use
oOther strategic site characteristics (brownfield redevelopment, urban center, smart 
growth, etc.)
oQuality of marketing plan
Track A – Addendum 
FAST NY Track B Grants will fund necessary costs to assist grantees in completing pre-development 
activities, such as those required for FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification. These activities are listed 
below. 
Track A – FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification  
ESD will award FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification to those applicants that satisfy the production 
requirement of the following documents, as applicable: 
State Historic Preservation Office (SHPO) consultation on plan 
Site plan and subdivision approval by local municipality 

Phase I Environmental Site Assessment (ESA) and/or Phase II ESA report 
Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) mapping 
Park rules, regulations and covenants document (created by developer) 
NYS Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC) Natural Heritage program 
correspondence and U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (USFWS) database search findings 
Environmental Assessment Form (EAF) and Negative Declaration or Draft & Final Environmental 
Impact Statement (FEIS)  
Site plan and boundary survey map 
Soil survey map 
Signoff by oversight body if needed for special district (e.g., Adirondack Park Agency, agricultural 
district, etc.) 
Preliminary storm water management plan 
Department of Transportation (DOT) review of traffic impact study 
Wetland avoidance and minimization plan – pre-application meeting with US Army Corps of 
Engineers and DEC 
Smart Growth Impact Statement 
Other necessary project approvals specific to site

FAST NY Quick Reference Guide
Deadlines Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis 
Eligibility Criteria Eligible Applicants include: 
Municipalities 
Municipally designated non-profit economic development organizations 
Municipally designated Local Development Corporation/Industrial Development Agency 
Site Condition Eligibility: 
Sites must be at least 40 acres in size OR demonstrate compelling site assets or public benefits 
Applicants must have either 
o Have ownership or control of the site, or 
o Written consent from the ownership entity 
Awards Track A – Shovel-Ready Certification 
 No-cost designation for site marketing purposes 
Track B – Pre-Development Grants 
Working capital grants up to $500,000 per site 
Awarded to fund costs of necessary pre-development site planning and reviews for future shovel-
ready sites 
Grants will fund necessary costs to complete pre-development activities, such as those required 
for FAST NY Shovel-Ready Certification 
50% non-ESD match required 
Track C—Infrastructure Improvements 
Capital grants awarded to improve the shovel-readiness of existing, mature sites 
Infrastructure improvements such as water, sewer, transportation, electricity, gas, and other 
capital-eligible site improvement costs can be funded 
Competitive grants will be available to sites that have substantially met the requirements of FAST 
NY Shovel-Ready certification 
A non-ESD match will be required, to be negotiated 
Eligible Uses of 
Funds Eligible Industries: 
High-tech manufacturing, including but need not be limited to clean- tech renewable energy, life 
sciences, agribusiness, optics, transportation equipment, materials processing, industrial 
machinery manufacturing and other advanced manufacturing  
Sites for interstate distribution and logistics  
Priority may be given to semiconductor manufacturing and related industry and supply-chain 
projects 
Eligible Site Activities: 
Manufacturing, research and development, office park, interstate distribution and logistics 
Intent to Apply A completed Letter of Intent to Apply must be submitted at https://esd.ny.gov/fast-ny . ESD will confirm 
receipt within 7 calendar days. A determination regarding your eligibility to submit the full application 
will be made within 30 calendar days. Full applications will not be accepted until applicants have 
received an approved Letter of Intent to Apply from ESD.  
Application Applications are accepted on a rolling basis through the CFA Portal
Once submitted, Empire State Development will review for completeness and score 
A non-refundable $250 application fee is required to apply. A wire transfer should be made to 
Empire State Development Corporation using the following information: 
Bank Name : JPMorgan Chase Bank 
Account Name : ESD ED Income 
ABA Number : 021 000 021 
Account Number : 007004796 
Reference : “[Applicant Name] – FAST NY Application Fee 
Awardees will be notified by email if awarded 
Questions For questions regarding this application, email fastny@esd.ny.gov  or contact your local ESD Regional 
Office

New York’s Investment Strategy
New York State drives economic growth by making investments that build on 
FOUR strategic pillars:
Placemaking is the belief that in order to attract a talented workforce and grow business, our built 
environment must emphasize creating accessible job centers, sustainable infrastructure and vibrant, 
livable communities. 
Workforce is the belief that people drive the economy. Targeted job training and education ensures that 
jobs in high -paying, in -demand, tradable sectors are filled in an equitable way.
Tradable Sectors: Investing in tradable sectors (both products and services) —industries that increase 
export -based employment opportunities —creates greater output and higher -paying jobs.
Innovation is the strategy of creating synergies between research and commercialization to achieve 
economic growth. Our investment focus on creating jobs of the future, sparks entrepreneurship and the 
birth of new businesses.

Objective
Under New York State’s POWER UP Grant Program, Empire State Development will provide an initial $100 million in grants to develop the electric infrastructure of sites statewide to create a network of power-ready sites —locations where upgraded electrical capacity significantly shortens the time between business site selection and operational launch.  By modernizing and expanding electric infrastructure, POWER UP enhances New York’s competitiveness in attracting high-quality jobs, particularly in advanced manufacturing. POWER UP will position New York State to attract high-growth industries through durable, future-facing upgrades that align with the energy and operational needs of 21st-century businesses.
Next Section
Continue
Overview
In FY26, Governor Kathy Hochul created the Promote Opportunity with Electric Readiness for Underdeveloped Properties (POWER UP) incentive program to aid New York State (NYS) in creating an inventory of turn-key industrial sites to attract investment from next-generation manufacturers. The increasing power demands of modern manufacturers signifies a commitment to cutting-edge technologies like advanced robotics and computing systems—important drivers of a 21st-century economy. Developing the level of electrical infrastructure needed to support these technologies and manufacturers is a complex undertaking but is a crucial investment to keep NYS competitive in an evolving economic landscape.
Building on the ongoing success of FAST NY, which provides grants to municipalities in New York to develop "shovel-ready" industrial sites, the new POWER UP program will focus on proactively developing electrical infrastructure at key industrial sites, helping to build a suite of locations that can support the next generation of modern manufacturers looking to operate in NYS. POWER UP will help to significantly shorten site development timelines and will allow New York to offer industrial sites that are both power-ready and shovel-ready.
Empire State Development (ESD) will administer POWER UP in consultation with the Department of Public Service (DPS). ESD will lead program administration and site evaluation. DPS will provide advisory input, leveraging its knowledge of utility capital planning to ensure that proposed infrastructure investments are technically sound and aligned with regulatory requirements and public interest objectives.
Next Section
Continue

to apply.

Eligibility
POWER UP Program Guidelines
Eligible Applicants:
Utilities, including:
Regulated Utility Companies
Public Power Authorities
Municipal Utilities
Electric Cooperatives
Other corporate structures as approved by ESD
Industrial Development Agencies (IDAs)
Municipalities
Local Development Corporations (LDCs)
Municipally Designated Economic Development Organizations (EDOs)
Applicants must either:
a. have ownership or control of the site, or
b. provide written consent from the ownership entity and, if different, the entity that has legal control of the site agreeing to use POWER UP funds as outlined in the application.
Eligible Sites:
Must be targeted to attract the following sectors:
a. high-tech manufacturing, including but not limited to semiconductors, renewable energy, life sciences, agribusiness, optics, transportation equipment, or materials processing.
b. Other priority sectors as determined by ESD.
Must be a minimum of 40 contiguous developable acres in size and demonstrate a high-level of readiness with respect to preliminary engineering, feasibility studies, and engagement with electric utility providers.
a. Waivers may be granted by ESD for smaller sites that demonstrate exceptional public benefits or strategic value, such as proximity to intermodal transportation, population centers, brownfield redevelopment opportunities, or critical infrastructure supporting underserved communities or statewide energy goals.
Industrial campuses with significant vacancy and underutilized infrastructure shall be eligible, even in the absence of new developable land, provided the existing structures can be adapted for use by an eligible strategic industry and require power-related upgrades to become viable.
Eligible Costs
Capital grants will be awarded to enhance the power readiness of mature industrial sites by funding critical infrastructure upgrades. Eligible improvements include, but are not necessarily limited to:
electrical infrastructure such as transformers, conductors, buses, switches, insulators, substations, and poles; and,
utility conduits, including conduit and duct banks;
technology and monitoring systems such as monitoring equipment, energy storage, and alternative energy system components
non-electrical infrastructure costs, such as masonry, fencing, excavation, structural fill, site restoration, and property acquisition, may also be eligible if essential for the implementation of the POWER UP project.
Other costs as determined by ESD.

1 
 September 18, 2025  Program Guidelines  
New York State  
Promote Opportunity with Electric 
Readiness for Underdeveloped Properties 
(POWER UP)  
Grant Program  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
State of New York  
Kathy Hochul,  Governor  
Empire State Development  
 
   


   
 
2 
 Table of Contents  
I. Overview  ................................ ................................ ................................ .....................  3 
II. Definitions  ................................ ................................ ................................ ..................  3 
III. Eligib ility Criteria  ................................ ................................ ................................ .......  4 
IV. Eligible Costs  ................................ ................................ ................................ ..............  5 
V. Application Instructions  ................................ ................................ ...........................  5 
VI. Documentation Requirements for Applicants  ................................ ......................  6 
VII. Awards  ................................ ................................ ................................ .........................  6 
VIII. Additional Award Criteria to be Considered by ESD  ................................ ............  7 
IX. Non-Discrimination and Contractor & Supplier Diversity  ................................ .......  7 
X. Additional Information  ................................ ................................ ...............................  7 
XI. Contact and Technical Support  ................................ ................................ ...............  7 
  

   
 
3 
 I. Overview  
 
In FY26, Governor Kathy Hochul created the  Promote Opportunity with Electric Readiness for 
Underdeveloped Properties (POWER UP) incentive program  to aid New York State  (NYS)  in 
creating an inventory of turn -key industrial sites to attract investment from next -generation 
manufacturers .  The increasing  power demands of modern manufacturers  signifies a 
commitment to cutting -edge technologies like advanced robotics  and computing systems —
important drivers  of a 21st -century economy.  Developing the level of electrical infrastructure 
needed to support these technologies and manufacturers is a complex  undertaking , but is a 
crucial investment to keep NYS competitive  in an evolving economic landscape . 
 
Building on the  ongoing  success of FAST NY , which provides grants to municipalities in New York 
to develop "shovel -ready" industrial sites , the new  POWER UP program will focus on proactively 
developing electrical infrastructure at key  industrial  sites, helping to build a suite of locations  
that can support the next generation of modern manufacturers looking to operate in NYS. 
POWER UP will help cut years off site development timelines and  will allow  New York to offer 
industrial sites that are both power -ready and shovel -ready . 
 
Empire State Development  (ESD) will administer POWER UP  in consultation with the Department 
of Public Service (DPS). ESD will lead program  administration  and site evaluation .  DPS will 
provide advisory input, leveraging its knowledge of utility capital planning to ensure that 
proposed infrastructure investments are technically sound  and aligned with regulatory 
requirements and public interest objectives.  
 
By preparing power -ready sites , POWER UP will position New York to attract high -growth 
industries through durable, future -facing upgrades that align with the energy and operational 
needs of 21st -century businesses.  
 
II. Definitions  
 
▪ Municipality shall mean an incorporated  municipal subdivision that is a county, city, 
town, or village in New York State .  
▪ Municipally Designated Non -Profit Economic Development Organization  shall mean 
New York State -based nonprofit industrial development agencies and authorities and 
economic development corporations designated to act on the behalf of the municipality 
in which the site is located.  
▪ Control of the Site  means the applicant must have legal authority to manage and 
develop the site for the purposes of the grant.  This may include fee ownership, 
leasehold interests with development rights, or formal authorization from the 
property owner.  Control must enable the applicant to implement capital 
improvements funded through the program.  
▪ Soft Costs  mean project -related professional fees and administrative costs incurred 
prior to or during construction, including architectural and engineering services, 

   
 
4 
 surveying, environmental assessments, permitting, legal, or project management 
expenses.  
▪ Mature Site  means a site that has undergone sufficient planning and due diligence to 
support near -term development.   This includes preliminary engineering, feasibility 
analysis, and demonstrated engagement with utility and regulatory agencies.  
 
III. Eligib ility Criteria   
 
Eligible Applicants include:  
o Utilities, including:   
▪ Regulated Utility Companies  
▪ Public Power Authorities  
▪ Municipal Utilities  
▪ Electric Cooperatives  
▪ Other corporate structures as approved by ESD  
o Industrial Development Agencies (IDAs)  
o Municipalities  
o Local Development Corporations (LDCs)  
o Municipally Designated Economic Development Organizations (EDOs)  
 
▪ Applicant s must either : 
a. have ownership or control of the site, or  
b. provide written consent from the ownership entity and, if different, the 
entity that has legal control of the site agreeing to use POWER UP funds 
as outlined in the application.  
 
Eligible Sites: 
 
▪ Must be targeted to attract the following sectors:  
a. high -tech manufacturing, including but not limited to semiconductors, 
renewable energy, life sciences, agribusiness, optics, transportation 
equipment, or materials processing .  
b. Other priority sectors as determined by ESD.  
 
▪ Must be a minimum of 40 contiguous developable acres in size and demonstrate 
a high -level of readiness with respect to  preliminary engineering,  feasibility 
studies, and engagement with electric utility providers . 
a. Waivers may be granted  by ESD  for smaller sites that demonstrate exceptional 
public benefits or strategic value, such as proximity to intermodal 
transportation, population centers, brownfield redevelopment opportunities, 
or critical infrastructure supporting underserved communities or  statewide 
energy goals.  
 

   
 
5 
 ▪ Industrial campuses with significant vacancy and underutilized infrastructure shall be 
eligible, even in the absence of new developable land, provided the existing structures 
can be adapted for  use by an eligible  strategic industry and require power -related 
upgrades to become viable.  
 
IV. Eligible Costs  
 
▪ Capital grants will be awarded to enhance the power readiness  of mature industrial 
sites by funding critical infrastructure upgrades. Eligible improvements include , but 
are not necessarily limited to:  
• electrical infrastructure such as transformers, conductors, buses, switches, 
insulators, substations, and poles; and,  
• utility conduits , including conduit and duct banks;  
• technology and monitoring systems such as monitoring equipment, energy 
storage, and alternative energy system components  
• non-electrical infrastructure costs , such as masonry, fencing, excavation, 
structural fill, site restoration, and property acquisition , may also be eligible 
if essential for the implementation of the POWER UP project.  
• Other costs as determined by ESD.  
 
V. Application Instructions  
 
Applications will be accepted on a rolling basis.  
 
a. Letter of Intent  
i. A "Letter of Intent" demonstrating an intent to apply is required. Information 
regarding the Letter of Intent is available online at https://esd.ny.gov/powerup .  
ii. ESD will confirm receipt by email within seven (7) calendar days.  
iii. Within 30 calendar days, ESD will advise the Applicant by email whether they may 
proceed with a full application.  
 
b. Application  
i. A non-refundable application fee of $250 is required. Documentation of this 
payment is required as part of the application.  
ii. Eligible Applicants must complete the application through the Consolidated 
Funding Application (CFA) online portal: https://apps.cio.ny.gov/apps/cfa/ .  
iii. Once submitted, ESD will review applications for completeness and subsequently 
score them.  
iv. Site visits may be completed before award selection.  
v. An interview with the project applicant may be required .  
vi. ESD reserves the right to request additional information to aid in the evaluation 
of an application.  
vii. Awardees will be notified by email.  
 

   
 
6 
 c. Commitment Fee  
i. A one percent (1%) non -reimbursable commitment fee based on the grant 
amount awarded will be assessed to all awardees.  
ii. The commitment fee will be due upon execution of the documents required for 
processing the award, following approval by the ESD Directors.  
iii. The Applicant will be obligated to pay for out -of-pocket expenses incurred by ESD 
in connection with the project, including, but not limited to, attorney fees, 
appraisals, surveys, title insurance, credit checks, filing fees, public hearing 
expenses, and o ther expenses deemed necessary by ESD.  
 
VI. Documentation Requirements  for Applicants  
 
This section outlines the documentation necessary to be considered for an award . Please note 
that additional documents may be requested  in order to complete ESD’s evaluation of 
applications.   
 
▪ Letter of support from the chief executive and/or legislative resolution  of the local 
Municipality that maintains land use  control over the proposed project site.  
▪ Documentation of site ownership or control or written consent from the ownership 
entity consenting to the application for POWER UP  funding.   
▪ Documented research  and/or market study showing the specific industry that will be 
targeted and the specific  electrical  infrastructure needs. Applicant must demonstrate 
that the requested electrical upgrades  will meet industry requirements  and are vital to 
attract ing the target industries .   
▪ Detailed c ost estimates for capital upgrades.  
▪ Documentation of engagement with local electrical utility providers  for needed 
infrastructure.   
▪ A detailed and documented plan for how infrastructure upgrade (s) will be accomplished  
is required , including , but not limited to:  
 
o Electrical capacity /load  study for the target area and associated 
infrastructure , performed in concert with the electrical utility . 
o Preliminary engineering , deliverables,  and engineer’s estimate of probable 
costs for the proposed improvement . 
o If required, applicants must demonstrate that they have initiated the NYISO 
System Impact Study process  per NYISO OATT Section 4.5.8 (Load 
Interconnection Procedures, incorporated via Manual 23) . This process shall 
be mandatory for load projects that either (a) connect at 115 kV or higher and 
exceed 10 MW, or (b) are 80 MW or greater , regardless of voltage level.  
o Documentation demonstrating the ultimate ownership, operational, and 
maintenanc e responsibilities for the new/improved infrastructure . 
o Survey and/or title to all easements and acquisitions for utility alignments , 
substations , or associated footprint  required to implement the improvement  
o Timeline for construction start and completion . 

   
 
7 
 o Documentation of e stimated lead times on equipment . 
o Plan of finance . 
▪ Other materials as may be required under a POWER UP  program application.  
 
VII. Awards  
 
▪ Capital grants will be awarded to improve the power -readiness of existing, mature 
sites by funding infrastructure improvements .  
▪ Soft costs are not to exceed 20% of total  costs, including final design, preparation of 
bid documents, legal fees, and regulatory fee requirements .  
▪ Awardees will  be required to demonstrate matching funds.  In addition to project 
readiness, priority will be given to applicants who demonstrate a higher percentage 
of matching sources.  
▪ Grants proceeds will be  made available on a reimbursement basis.  
 
VIII. Additional Award Criteria to be Considered by ESD  
 
In addition to meeting documentation requirements outlined in Section VI of the Program 
Guidelines, ESD will consider the following criteria when evaluating applications .   
 
▪ Demonstration of industry interest , including the site meeting the target power 
requirements thereof . 
▪ Demonstration of financial viability.  
▪ Alignment with R egional Economic Development Council’s  regional economic 
development strategy.  
▪ Leveraged investment, such as private and local investment.  
▪ Tenant commitments, such as  signed letters of interest and/or contributions.  
▪ Documentation of substantial site development progress , indicating a readiness for 
POWER UP investment . 
▪ Maturity of project as determined in consultation with DPS .  
 
IX. Non -Discrimination and Contractor & Supplier Diversity  
 
▪ Under New York State Executive Law Articles 15 -A and Article 3 of New York State 
Veterans' Services Law, ESD recognizes its obligation under the law to promote 
opportunities for maximum feasible participation of certified minority and women -owned 
business enterprises (MWBEs) and service -disabled veteran -owne d businesses (SDVOBs) 
in the performance of ESD projects. The Recipient shall be required to use Good Faith 
Efforts (under 5 NYCRR §142.8 and 9 NYCRR § 252.2(n)) to achieve MWBE and SDVOB 
Participation. Additionally, ESD's MWBE and SDVOB Participation/Equa l Employment 
Opportunity Policy will apply to this project. While ESD’s agency -wide MWBE participation 
goal is 30% and the SDVOB participation goal is 6%, specific goals related to the total value 
of ESD's funding will be established on a grant -by-grant ba sis. 
 

   
 
8 
 X. Additional Information  
 
▪ Up to $ 300 million will be made available for capital grant funding , subject to 
appropriation . 
▪ Awards will be allocated on a rolling basis until funding is exhausted.  
▪ ESD may consider regional or geographic parity in allocating awards.  
▪ ESD may require restrictions on the future use of awarded sites consistent with the 
purposes of their POWER UP grant terms.  
 
XI. Contact and Technical Support  
 
For more information about POWER UP program guidelines or to begin the  application 
process , please email: powerup@esd.ny.gov

Eligible Uses of Funds  
• Eligible industries : 
o High -tech manufacturing, including but not limited to semiconductors, renewable 
energy, life sciences, agribusiness, optics, transportation equipment, or materials 
processing.  
o Other priority sectors as determined by ESD.  
• All grant funds will be issued on a reimbursement basis  
• The Recipient shall be required to use “Good Faith Efforts,” to achieve a Minority and Women -
owned Business Enterprise (“MWBE”) participation goal : 
o Pursuant to New York State Executive Law Articles 15 -A and Article 3 of New York State 
Veterans’ Services Law, ESD recognizes its obligation under the law to promote 
opportunities for maximum feasible participation of certified New York State minority and 
women -owned business enterprises (MWBEs) and service -disabled veteran -owned 
businesses (SDVOBs) in the performance of ESD projects.   The Recipient shall be required 
to use Good Faith Efforts (pursuant to 5 NYCRR §142.8 and 9 NYCRR § 252.2(n) to achieve 
MWB E and SDVOB Participation. In addition, ESD’s MWBE and SDVOB Participation/Equal 
Employment Opportunity Policy will apply to this program. Specific goals related to the 
total value of ESD’s funding will be established on a grant -by grant basis.  
o ESD has an agency -wide goal of 30% for MWBE participation, 15% for New York State -
certified Minority -owned Business Enterprise (“MBE”) participation and 15% for New 
York State -certified Women -owned Business Enterprise (“WBE”) participation (based on 

 
the current availability of MBEs and WBEs). ESD has an agency -wide goal of 6% for SDVOB 
participation, based on the current availability of qualified SDVOBs.  
o To allow ESD Office of Contractor Supplier Diversity (OCSD) to properly assess 
MWBE and SDVOB utilization goals on utility work, please use the Project Budget 
Summary of Good Faith Efforts  template to itemize your anticipated project costs.  
Scoring  Criteria  
• The following will be evaluated as a part of the basic application scoring criteria  
o Demonstration of financial viability  
o Quality of information and documentation for the proposed plan  
o Alignment with REDC strategy  
o Favorable site characteristics (topography, developability, useable acreage, etc)  
o Highway and truck access to meet needs of intended use  
o Utility services to site to meet needs of intended use  
o Other strategic site characteristics ( brownfield redevelopment, urban center, smart 
growth, etc.)  
o Quality of marketing plan  
o Demonstration of industry interest (ie. studies, leads , tenant interest/commitments ) 
o Leveraged investment (match)  
o Applicant’s development experience

Eligible Industries:

sectors:

POWER UP | ELIGIBLE SITES | 14 Eligible Sites:
•Must be a minimum of 40 contiguous developable 
acres in size and demonstrate a high -level of readiness 
with respect to preliminary engineering, feasibility studies, 
and engagement with electric utility providers.
•a. Waivers may be granted by ESD for smaller sites 
that demonstrate exceptional public benefits or 
strategic value
•Industrial campuses with significant vacancy 
and underutilized infrastructure shall be eligible, 
even in the absence of new developable land, 
provided the existing structures can be adapted for 
use by an eligible strategic industry and require 
power -related upgrades to become viable.

Eligible Costs:
•Capital grants will be awarded to enhance the power readiness of 
mature industrial sites by funding critical infrastructure upgrades. 
Eligible improvements include, but are not necessarily limited to:
•electrical infrastructure such as transformers, conductors, 
buses, switches, insulators, substations, and poles; and,
•utility conduits, including conduit and duct banks; 
•technology and monitoring systems such as monitoring 
equipment, energy storage, and alternative energy system 
components 
•non-electrical infrastructure costs, such as masonry, 
fencing, excavation, structural fill, site restoration, and 
property acquisition, may also be eligible if essential for the 
implementation of the POWER UP project.
•Other costs as determined by ESD.Eligible Costs

Objective
Under New York’s County Infrastructure Grant Program, Empire State Development will provide up to $100 million in grants to support county-led infrastructure projects across the State, with enhanced awards for projects supporting the creation of housing.
Next Section
Continue
Overview
The Program intentionally focuses on smaller-scale infrastructure projects which may lack standalone funding sources, but nevertheless are priority capital projects for counties. The Program is designed to invest in projects that: contribute to placemaking, support the creation of housing, and encourage visitation and tourism.
Next Section
Continue
Program Status
*As of December 2025
The County Infrastructure Grant Program was initially launched in November 2024, and the
first round
of grants was awarded in July 2025. Learn more about these first round awards
here
.
A
second round
of applications was
recently announced
.
A CIGP Round 2 webinar was held on December 18, 2025.
Final Application Due Date: April 1, 2026
Next Section
Continue
Eligibility
Eligible Applicants
Counties located in NYS, excluding the counties of Kings, Queens, Richmond, Bronx, and New York.
Eligible Projects
Small and moderate-scale priority infrastructure projects identified by the county which directly or indirectly support economic development, support the creation of housing, contribute to placemaking, or encourage tourism.
Eligible Uses of Funds
Construction, expansion, and/or rehabilitation of municipal, utility, or other public property and/or ancillary infrastructure to support such developments;
Extension or reconstruction of road, water, sewer, or utilities;
Design or engineering costs related to construction (limited to 20% of total project cost);
Acquisition of machinery and equipment related to infrastructure projects;
Placemaking and beautification project costs;
Site preparation, demolition, acquisition of real property related to the county-led infrastructure project; and
Other capital uses as determined by Empire State Development (ESD)
Grant Funding
All projects that do not directly support the creation of at least (10) ten new housing units, are eligible for an award up to $500,000.
All projects that directly support the creation of at least (10) ten new housing units, are eligible for an award up to $1,000,000.
Minimum 50% non-ESD match required
A non-refundable $250 application fee will be required
Next Section
Continue
How to Apply
Program Guidelines
Letter of Intent
– Counties are required to submit a
Letter of Intent
to ESD, to describe the proposed project and determine eligibility, prior to a full application. A Letter of Intent should be sent to the email below, with a subject line of “County Name – Letter of Intent.” Please allow (4) weeks between submission of a Letter of Intent and a finalized application.
Applications
– Will be accepted on a rolling basis through rounds, in the
CFA Portal
.
Awards will be allocated through rounds, until funding is exhausted.
Only one application and one grant are permitted per county, each round.
Any unspent funds will be redeployed in future rounds, at the discretion of ESD.
